question: HOW DOES THe OIL AFFECT ME AND MY BALL REACTION?
content:
Oil affects you because it changes when and how your ball starts to read the lane.
The more oil there is, the longer the ball will slide before reacting.
The less oil there is, the sooner the ball will grab the lane and start to hook.
This matters because it directly affects your miss room, and your carry:
	More oil → the ball skids longer, hooks later, and usually becomes harder to control if your speed or hand position isn’t consistent.
	Less oil → the ball finds friction earlier, so it hooks sooner.
Understanding where the oil is helps you choose the right target, ball, and release, so your ball reacts where you expect it to, not earlier, not later.
That’s how you keep control of your shot and improve your scores.
